title: [FIRRTL] Property flow checking should ensure we can't assign an output to an output
body:
Here's the simplest example I came up with:

```
FIRRTL version 3.3.0
circuit Top :
  module Top :
    skip

  class MyClass :
    output out : Integer
    propassign out, out
```

Currently, this makes it to LowerClasses and then triggers an assert when we go to erase `MyClass`, because `out` still has uses (the `om.class.field` that the output propassign is converted to).

```
Assertion failed: (use_empty() && "Cannot destroy a value that still has uses!"), function ~IRObjectWithUseList, file UseDefLists.h, line 198.
```

I'm not sure where in the stack this flow checking should occur, but even handling it later in LowerClasses would at least give a better error message than the current one.
